records.ety: [LL. contravenire; L. contra + venire to come: cf. F. contrevenir. See Come.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Contravened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Contravening.]

defs: To meet in the way of opposition; to come into conflict with; to oppose; to contradict; to obstruct the operation of; to defeat.
quotes: So plain a proposition . . . was not likely to be contravened.

defs: To violate; to nullify; to be inconsistent with; as, to contravene a law.
quotes: Laws that place the subjects in such a state contravene the first principles of the compact of authority.
